mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate cash flows, directly depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in > 55% of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in consumer price inflation and deceleration in retail sales indicates stagflationary pressure, forcing central banks to tighten policy while corporate margins compress, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a positive Sharpe ratio or Rank IC > 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and robust economic activity, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth periods show negative median forward returns or t-statistic < 1.5 over 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year CPI inflation exceeds 5.0%, market participants price in aggressive monetary tightening and higher equity risk premiums, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market expansion, boosting corporate revenue expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces a negative information coefficient or positive returns occur in less than 45% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in housing starts reflect strong consumer confidence and rising demand for construction materials, transmitting positive earnings revisions to the S&P 500 over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over any 3-year rolling window or signal direction is uncorrelated with forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth falls below 1.0% annually, corporate earnings forecasts are downgraded due to weakening aggregate demand, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns exceed zero in more than 55% of quarters where GDPC1 growth falls below the threshold.
